item.name: Sessions
item.description: WhatsApp session lifecycle (create, list, connect, pair, delete).

description.content:
Returns every non-deleted session in the account when called with a
PAT. When called with a session-scoped key, returns only that one
bound session (one-element list).
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Creates a new WhatsApp session. The response includes the one-time
raw `session_api_key.raw` (`bps_sk_*`) — store it; the API never
returns it again.

item.name: Messages
item.description: Send, edit, delete, resend, inspect WhatsApp messages.

description.content:
Discriminated by `kind` (and presence of media-URL / text / location
/ contact / poll fields). Requires an `Idempotency-Key` header.

For session-scoped keys (`bps_sk_*`), passing `session_id` in the
body is rejected with 422 if it doesn't match the bound session.
PATs may pass `session_id` explicitly.
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

Send a message to a WhatsApp Channel / Community newsletter. Use the channel JID ending in `@newsletter` as `channel_id`.

variable.description: Wapio API base URL. Override per environment.
Bearer token: `bps_pat_*` (PAT) or `bps_sk_*` (session-scoped). Prefer setting this in an environment, not the collection itself.
WhatsApp session id (bps_sess_ws_*) used in path params.
Message id (bps_msg_*) used in /api/messages/* path params.
